deployment.name: Public geospatial Q&A (example)
deployment.description: Public-facing conversational interface to Earth-observation datasets. Authenticated researchers and the public ask natural-language questions; the agent plans the data lookup, runs statistics over rasters, and answers in plain language.

anchor_query.example: Did monthly NO2 over New York City change from October to December 2021?

_calibration.sample_size: 120 user-turns (6 turns × 20 repeats); 238 LLM calls including tool-loop round-trips

intervals_across_sessions.comment: Across 13 cleanly-detected cold-cache session boundaries within the N=20 run; the aggregate cache rate (0.88) is at the upper edge of the per-session CI because later repeats inherit warm-cache state from earlier ones (OpenAI's auto-prefix cache persists across requests).

_calibration.accuracy_statement: Within ±5% on every measured coefficient. Per-session cache-rate stdev is 4.3 percentage points (RSD ≈5%) — tight distribution. Switch to Moderate or Heavy below for higher-payload variants — those are estimated, not measured.

diagnostics.comment: On average each user turn triggers one tool round-trip (gating tools: parse_datetime, geocode, search_collections). compute_stats + build_viz_tiles run together in a single non-gating turn at the end, which is why the loop multiplier stays near 2× rather than 3×. Stable across N=3 (2.00) and N=20 (1.98) runs.

minimal.label: Minimal (validated)
minimal.description: ReAct agent with TEMPLATED tool returns: bbox-only geocode, minimal collection-option summaries, server-side state for items and stats. Per-turn input stays roughly flat across turns because tool responses don't compound in history. Measured against real OpenAI gpt-5.2 on 2026-05-13 (20 repeats × 6 turns, 238 LLM calls, $0.21 spend; per-session cache-rate stdev 4.3 pp).

moderate.label: Moderate (estimated)
moderate.description: Full STAC item metadata for top-5 results + admin-geometry geocode payload + summary statistics. Closer to what a production deployment injects when retrieval is well-condensed but unabridged.

heavy.label: Heavy (upper bound)
heavy.description: Full raster previews, complete admin geometry, dense STAC catalog snapshots, multi-collection comparison. Estimated upper bound — if you're hitting this, your retrieval pipeline likely needs condensation.

_calibration.notes: Token shape was measured from a 7-stage ReAct agent (parse_datetime → geocode → search_collections → select_collection → search_items → compute_stats → build_viz_tiles) with templated tool responses. A real production deployment that injects full STAC item metadata + raster summaries per query will run higher — likely 10-30K input tokens depending on how aggressively retrieval payloads are condensed before they enter the LLM context. The pre-calibration value (84,490) was a conservative upper bound from a freeform-tool model; switching the bench to templated-response mode (matching what production agents do via a centralized response-template layer) measured 3,376 input tokens. Production teams should re-run the bench against their actual retrieval pipeline.

mixed.label: Mixed (40% full · 30% rag · 15% partial · 10% refusal · 5% heavy)

phases.label: Year 1 — pilot on API
phases.description: Low-risk start. Validate workload + cache hit rate against real usage before committing to spend.

phases.label: Year 2 — committed-spend reservation
phases.description: Lock in a discount with Azure PTU or OpenAI Enterprise commit once volume is steady.

phases.label: Year 3 — hybrid 50/50
phases.description: Stand up Llama-based self-host for steady baseline traffic; keep API for spikes and tail queries.
